FFmpeg — bundled binary (vendor/ffmpeg/ffmpeg.exe)
===================================================

VibeTools bundles a prebuilt FFmpeg binary, which it invokes as a SEPARATE
PROCESS (it is never linked into VibeTools' own code). VibeTools itself is
licensed under the MIT License (see ../../LICENSE). FFmpeg is a separate work
distributed here under its own license, summarized below.

Bundled build
-------------
  Version:   ffmpeg 6.1.1 ("essentials" build)
  Source of build: gyan.dev  — https://www.gyan.dev/ffmpeg/builds/
  Build config: --enable-gpl --enable-version3 (and others; run `ffmpeg -version`
                for the full configuration string)

License
-------
Because this build was configured with --enable-gpl --enable-version3, the
binary is licensed under the GNU General Public License, version 3 (GPLv3).

  FFmpeg is free software; you can redistribute it and/or modify it under the
  terms of the GNU General Public License as published by the Free Software
  Foundation; either version 3 of the License, or (at your option) any later
  version.

Full license text: https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt

Corresponding source code
--------------------------
The complete corresponding source code for FFmpeg is available from:

  - FFmpeg project source:  https://ffmpeg.org/download.html  (and
    https://git.ffmpeg.org/ffmpeg.git — check out tag n6.1.1)
  - Build recipe / source for this specific build (gyan.dev):
    https://www.gyan.dev/ffmpeg/builds/

Note for redistributors / forks
--------------------------------
If you prefer to avoid GPL obligations, you may replace this binary with an
LGPL build of FFmpeg, or obtain it at install time via the `ffmpeg-static` npm
package. VibeTools only requires that an `ffmpeg.exe` is present at
vendor/ffmpeg/ffmpeg.exe; the specific build is your choice.

FFmpeg is a trademark of Fabrice Bellard, originator of the FFmpeg project.
VibeTools is not affiliated with or endorsed by the FFmpeg project.
